landingTitle: Create a room or join one
landingIntro: Draw a scenario together around one shared table.
landingNote: Private by default. Rooms are temporary.
joinRoomMissingMessage: Use the shared room code or room link from the host.
turnHelpDeal: {player}: drag one top card onto the table and turn it over.
turnHelpDealPending: {player}: turn over the drawn card and read it aloud.
turnHelpDiscuss: Discuss how the cards shape the scenario. When you are ready, start the replacement round.
turnHelpReplace: {player}: replace one table card with a new card from the same deck and turn it over.
turnHelpReplacePending: {player}: turn over the new card and reshape the story accordingly.
timerFinishedMessage: You can keep talking or continue to the next turn.
oneCardTitle: Choose only one card per round.
oneCardMessage: Press Start over to get new options.
notYourTurnMessage: The current player is shown in the round panel.
discussionPhaseMessage: First describe the scenario using the six cards.
flipPendingCardTitle: Turn over the drawn card first.
flipPendingCardMessage: The turn advances only after the card on the table has been turned over.
activeCardOnlyTitle: Choose the top card of the deck.
activeCardOnlyMessage: The round advances one new card at a time.
wrongDropzoneTitle: Move the card to its matching place.
wrongDropzoneMessage: Each deck has its own place on the table.
dropzoneOccupiedTitle: There is already a card here.
dropzoneOccupiedMessage: In the first round, fill the empty places.
replaceExistingCardMessage: During the replacement round, drag the new card onto the old one.
notDiscussionPhaseTitle: The replacement round cannot start yet.
notDiscussionPhaseMessage: First fill the table with six cards and discuss the scenario.
confirmResetMessage: This will clear the shared table for everyone in the room.
confirmDealMessage: This will replace the current shared table for everyone in the room.
confirmLeaveMessage: You will return to the landing screen and disconnect from the current table.
confirmCloseRoomMessage: This closes the room for everyone and returns all players to the landing screen.
hostOnlyTitle: Only the host can do that.
hostOnlyMessage: The first player in the room controls reset, random deal, timer, and round changes.
roomClosedMessage: {player} closed the room. You are back on the landing screen.
roomClosedByYouMessage: The room is closed and everyone has returned to the landing screen.
invalidPayloadTitle: That action could not be understood.
invalidPayloadMessage: Please try again from the current game view.
tooManyRequestsMessage: Too many actions were sent too quickly.
roomFullMessage: The room has reached its maximum number of players.
roomExpiredTitle: This room is no longer available.
roomExpiredMessage: Create a new room or ask the host for an updated link.
serverBusyTitle: No new rooms can be created right now.
serverBusyMessage: Please try again in a moment.
serverErrorTitle: Something went wrong on the server.
serverErrorMessage: Please try the action again in a moment.
copyResultsSuccessMessage: You can paste the current scenario anywhere.
copyResultsEmptyTitle: There is nothing to copy yet.
copyResultsEmptyMessage: Place cards on the table first.
connectionLostMessage: Trying to reconnect to the room.
reconnectedMessage: Your previous player session was restored.
resolutionRotateMessage: Sattuma works best on a landscape tablet or computer.
resolutionTitle: The game table needs more space
resolutionMessage: Enlarge the browser window. Recommended size is at least 900 x 620 pixels.
mobileTitle: Open Sattuma on a larger landscape screen
mobileIntro1: Sattuma is a card game where six decks, card places, and the shared table need room on the screen.
mobileIntro2: The game works best on a computer or tablet in landscape orientation. The recommended size is at least 900 x 620 pixels.
mobileObserverEmpty: No table cards have been turned over yet.
mobileGameIdeaText: Draw situations, spaces, teaching formats, resources, and chance events from the decks. The cards help build a conversation around teaching, art, and digital tools.
mobileTogetherText: With the room code, several participants can follow the same round. Everyone can open their own view and play at the same table.
mobileMoreInfoText1: Sattuma was developed as part of the Taiteet ja digi project funded by the Finnish Ministry of Education and Culture, in collaboration between Uniarts Helsinki and Aalto University.
mobileMoreInfoText2: The game was designed by Tomi Slotte Dufva from the Aalto University School of Arts, Design and Architecture, together with Mikael Brygger and Tomi Humalisto from the Theatre Academy of Uniarts Helsinki.
mobileMoreInfoText3: The web game visuals and functionality were programmed by Tiago Martins Pinto from Aalto University.
